cot_prefix: Okay, let's tackle this question. The passage is about the London Olympics and how they were considered the "greenest" Games ever. The main focus is on the environmental efforts London made during the event. First, I'll look at the options given: (A) How to deal with the anti-doping (B) How to use recycled materials (C) London's green games (D) London's public transport The passage starts by mentioning that London could win a gold medal for hosting the "greenest" Games ever. It then goes on to discuss various green initiatives like cleaning up polluted areas, using recycled materials in construction, green building techniques, waste management, public transport usage, and even mentions anti-doping efforts as part of the greenness. Option A is about anti-doping, which is mentioned in the last paragraph but isn't the main focus of the entire passage. The anti-doping part is just one part of the green efforts, not the main theme. Option B talks about using recycled materials. While the passage does discuss using recycled materials in the construction of the Olympic Park and Stadium, this is just one of several green strategies mentioned. The title needs to cover the overall theme, not just one specific strategy. Option C, "London's green games," seems to encapsulate the entire theme. The passage is about how London made the Olympics environmentally friendly through various measures. The first paragraph even states that one thing is certain is that London could win a gold medal for the "greenest" Games ever, which directly supports this title. Option D is about public transport. The passage mentions using public transport as a way to reduce carbon emissions, but again, this is just one
